title: Dynamic Decision Learning: Test-Time Evolution for Abnormality Grounding in Rare Diseases
summary: Clinical abnormality grounding for rare diseases is often hindered by data scarcity, making supervised fine-tuning impractical and single-pass inference highly unstable. We propose Dynamic Decision Learning (DDL), a framework that enables frozen large vision-language models (LVLMs) to refine their decisions across both language and visual spaces by optimizing instructions and consolidating predictions under visual perturbations. This process improves localization quality and produces a consensus-based reliability score that quantifies model confidence. Results on brain imaging benchmarks, including a rare-disease dataset with 281 pathology types across models ranging from 3B to 72B parameters, show that DDL improves mAP@75 by up to 105% on rare-disease cases and outperforms adaptation baselines and supervised fine-tuning. Furthermore, DDL demonstrates stronger calibration between reliability scores and localization accuracy under severe distribution shifts and increasing task difficulty. Code is available at: https://lijunrio.github.io/DDL/

AI.tldr: 提出动态决策学习（DDL）框架，通过语言和视觉空间中的指令优化与预测整合，使冻结的大型视觉-语言模型能够在无需监督微调的情况下，显著提升罕见疾病影像中异常定位的准确性与可靠性。
AI.motivation: 临床异常定位任务中，罕见疾病常面临数据稀缺问题，导致有监督微调难以实施，单次推理极不稳定，亟需一种无需额外训练数据即可提升定位质量的方法。
AI.method: 提出动态决策学习（DDL）框架，在冻结的大型视觉-语言模型（LVLM）上，通过优化指令以提升语言空间中的决策质量，并在视觉扰动下整合多次预测结果，最终生成基于共识的可靠性分数以量化模型置信度。
AI.result: 在脑部影像基准测试及包含281种病理类型的罕见疾病数据集上，DDL在3B至72B参数模型上使mAP@75提升高达105%，超越多种适应基线和有监督微调方法；且在严重分布偏移和任务难度增加时，可靠性分数与定位精度之间呈现更强的校准性。
AI.conclusion: DDL证明，通过动态决策优化和预测整合，冻结的LVLM也能在数据稀缺的罕见疾病场景下实现高效且稳健的异常定位，其内置的可靠性分数为模型置信度提供了有效量化手段，对高风险临床部署具有重要价值。

title: Learning from Medical Entity Trees: An Entity-Centric Medical Data Engineering Framework for MLLMs
summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have shown transformative potential in medical applications, yet their performance is hindered by conventional data curation strategies that rely on coarse-grained partitioning by modality or department. Such fragmented approaches fail to capture the hierarchical and interconnected nature of clinical medical knowledge, limiting the models' ability to perform fine-grained recognition and complex reasoning. In this paper, we propose a novel Entity-Centric Medical Data Engineering framework. We automatically extract entities from authoritative medical literature to construct a Medical Entity Tree (MET), a hierarchical structure that systematically encodes diseases, anatomical structures, modalities, and symptoms into a unified knowledge repository. Building upon the MET, we propose an advanced data engine that includes: (1) node-guided retrieval to anchor raw data to specific medical concepts, (2) a two-stage hybrid filtering and alignment pipeline to ensure precise visual-semantic correspondence, and (3) knowledge-aware data synthesis to generate enriched captions and targeted reasoning VQA pairs, leveraging structural constraints. Extensive evaluations across six medical benchmarks demonstrate that our approach significantly enhances the medical capabilities of general-purpose MLLMs, improving their ability to handle complex clinical queries and achieve state-of-the-art performance in diverse medical contexts.

AI.tldr: 提出了一种以实体为中心的医学数据工程框架，通过构建医学实体树（MET）并集成节点引导检索、两阶段混合过滤对齐和知识感知数据合成三种机制，显著提升了通用多模态大语言模型在六个医学基准上的表现，实现了细粒度识别与复杂临床推理能力的突破。
AI.motivation: 现有医学多模态大语言模型采用粗粒度的按模态或科室划分的数据策展策略，未能捕捉临床医学知识的分层和互联特性，限制了模型在细粒度识别和复杂推理上的能力。
AI.method: 提出Entity-Centric医学数据工程框架，首先从权威医学文献中自动提取实体构建分层结构的医学实体树（MET），统一编码疾病、解剖结构、模态和症状；在此基础上设计数据引擎，包含(1)节点引导检索将原始数据锚定到具体医学概念，(2)两阶段混合过滤与对齐流水线确保精准的视觉-语义对应，(3)利用结构约束的知识感知数据合成生成富文本描述和定向推理VQA对。
AI.result: 在六个医学基准上的广泛评估表明，该方法显著增强了通用多模态大语言模型的医学能力，提升了其处理复杂临床查询的性能，并在多种医学场景中达到了最先进水平。
AI.conclusion: 该研究证实了通过结构化知识引导的细粒度数据工程能够有效弥合通用MLLM在医学领域的性能瓶颈，为医学AI中的知识表示与数据策展提供了新的范式，未来可拓展至更多垂直领域。

title: DV-World: Benchmarking Data Visualization Agents in Real-World Scenarios
summary: Real-world data visualization (DV) requires native environmental grounding, cross-platform evolution, and proactive intent alignment. Yet, existing benchmarks often suffer from code-sandbox confinement, single-language creation-only tasks, and assumption of perfect intent. To bridge these gaps, we introduce DV-World, a benchmark of 260 tasks designed to evaluate DV agents across real-world professional lifecycles. DV-World spans three domains: DV-Sheet for native spreadsheet manipulation including chart and dashboard creation as well as diagnostic repair; DV-Evolution for adapting and restructuring reference visual artifacts to fit new data across diverse programming paradigms and DV-Interact for proactive intent alignment with a user simulator that mimics real-world ambiguous requirements. Our hybrid evaluation framework integrates Table-value Alignment for numerical precision and MLLM-as-a-Judge with rubrics for semantic-visual assessment. Experiments reveal that state-of-the-art models achieve less than 50% overall performance, exposing critical deficits in handling the complex challenges of real-world data visualization. DV-World provides a realistic testbed to steer development toward the versatile expertise required in enterprise workflows. Our data and code are available at \href{https://github.com/DA-Open/DV-World}{this project page}.

AI.tldr: DV-World是一个包含260个任务的数据可视化基准，覆盖电子表格操作、跨平台演化与主动意图对齐三大真实场景。实验表明，现有最先进模型的综合性能低于50%，揭示了在复杂企业级可视化工作流中的严重不足。
AI.motivation: 现有数据可视化基准受限于代码沙箱环境、仅支持单语言创建型任务且假设用户意图完美表达，无法评估智能体在真实世界中的跨平台演化、环境交互与意图对齐能力。论文旨在填补真实数据可视化生命周期评估的空白。
AI.method: 构建DV-World基准，包含260个任务并划分为三个领域：DV-Sheet专注于原生电子表格操作（图表/仪表盘创建与诊断修复），DV-Evolution评估跨编程范式的参考可视化工件重构与适配，DV-Interact通过用户模拟器实现模糊需求下的主动意图对齐。采用混合评估框架，结合Table-value Alignment进行数值精度评估与基于MLLM-as-a-Judge的语义视觉评估。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，当前最先进的模型在DV-World上的整体性能低于50%，在三个子领域中都暴露出关键缺陷，尤其在面对真实企业工作流中复杂的可视化需求时表现不佳。
AI.conclusion: DV-World作为一个贴近实际企业工作流的真实测试平台，揭示了现有模型在数据可视化任务中的严重不足，为未来研究向具备多功能专业能力的方向发展提供了引导方向。

title: Toward Multimodal Conversational AI for Age-Related Macular Degeneration
summary: Despite strong performance of deep learning models in retinal disease detection, most systems produce static predictions without clinical reasoning or interactive explanation. Recent advances in multimodal large language models (MLLMs) integrate diagnostic predictions with clinically meaningful dialogue to support clinical decision-making and patient counseling. In this study, OcularChat, an MLLM, was fine-tuned from Qwen2.5-VL using simulated patient-physician dialogues to diagnose age-related macular degeneration (AMD) through visual question answering on color fundus photographs (CFPs). A total of 705,850 simulated dialogues paired with 46,167 CFPs were generated to train OcularChat to identify key AMD features and produce reasoned predictions. OcularChat demonstrated strong classification performance in AREDS, achieving accuracies of 0.954, 0.849, and 0.678 for the three diagnostic tasks: advanced AMD, pigmentary abnormalities, and drusen size, significantly outperforming existing MLLMs. On AREDS2, OcularChat remained the top-performing method on all tasks. Across three independent ophthalmologist graders, OcularChat achieved higher mean scores than a strong baseline model for advanced AMD (3.503 vs. 2.833), pigmentary abnormalities (3.272 vs. 2.828), drusen size (3.064 vs. 2.433), and overall impression (2.978 vs. 2.464) on a 5-point clinical grading rubric. Beyond strong objective performance in AMD severity classification, OcularChat demonstrated the ability to provide diagnostic reasoning, clinically relevant explanations, and interactive dialogue, with high performance in subjective ophthalmologist evaluation. These findings suggest that MLLMs may enable accurate, interpretable, and clinically useful image-based diagnosis and classification of AMD.

AI.tldr: OcularChat是一种基于Qwen2.5-VL微调的多模态大语言模型，通过705,850条模拟医患对话与46,167张眼底彩照的训练，在AMD诊断分类任务上显著优于现有MLLM，并在眼科医生主观评估中展现出优越的临床推理与交互对话能力。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习模型在视网膜疾病检测中表现出色，但大多只能提供静态预测，缺乏临床推理过程与交互式解释能力，限制了其在临床决策支持和患者咨询中的实际应用价值。
AI.method: 研究基于Qwen2.5-VL多模态大语言模型进行微调，构建OcularChat；通过生成705,850条模拟医患对话与46,167张眼底彩照的配对数据，训练模型对年龄相关性黄斑变性（AMD）进行视觉问答，识别关键AMD特征并输出带有推理过程的预测结果。
AI.result: 在AREDS数据集上，OcularChat在晚期AMD（0.954）、色素异常（0.849）和玻璃膜疣大小（0.678）三个诊断任务中准确率显著优于现有MLLM；在AREDS2数据集上同样保持最优性能。在5分制临床评分中，OcularChat在晚期AMD（3.503 vs. 2.833）、色素异常（3.272 vs. 2.828）、玻璃膜疣大小（3.064 vs. 2.433）及总体印象（2.978 vs. 2.464）上均高于基线模型。
AI.conclusion: 研究表明，多模态大语言模型能够在AMD影像诊断与分类中实现准确、可解释且具有临床实用性的预测，兼具诊断推理、临床相关解释和交互式对话能力，有望推动可解释的AI辅助眼科诊疗发展。

title: M$^3$-VQA: A Benchmark for Multimodal, Multi-Entity, Multi-Hop Visual Question Answering
summary: We present M$^3$-VQA, a novel knowledge-based Visual Question Answering (VQA) benchmark, to enhance the evaluation of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) in fine-grained multimodal entity understanding and complex multi-hop reasoning. Unlike existing VQA datasets that focus on coarse-grained categories and simple reasoning over single entities, M$^3$-VQA introduces diverse multi-entity questions involving multiple distinct entities from both visual and textual sources. It requires models to perform both sequential and parallel multi-hop reasoning across multiple documents, supported by traceable, detailed evidence and a curated multimodal knowledge base. We evaluate 16 leading MLLMs under three settings: without external knowledge, with gold evidence, and with retrieval-augmented input. The poor results reveal significant challenges for MLLMs in knowledge acquisition and reasoning. Models perform poorly without external information but improve markedly when provided with precise evidence. Furthermore, reasoning-aware agentic retrieval surpasses heuristic methods, highlighting the importance of structured reasoning for complex multimodal understanding. M$^3$-VQA presents a more challenging evaluation for advancing the multimodal reasoning capabilities of MLLMs. Our code and dataset are available at https://github.com/CASIA-IVA-Lab/M3VQA.

AI.tldr: M$^3$-VQA是一个面向多模态大语言模型的新型知识驱动视觉问答基准，引入了多实体、多跳推理任务，要求模型在跨视觉和文本来源的多文档上进行顺序和并行推理，实验表明现有MLLMs在该任务上表现不佳，需外部知识辅助和结构化推理策略才能显著提升性能。
AI.motivation: 现有VQA数据集多聚焦于粗粒度类别和基于单一实体的简单推理，缺乏对多实体、多文档交叉推理能力的评测，无法有效评估多模态大语言模型在细粒度多模态实体理解和复杂多跳推理方面的真实能力。
AI.method: 构建了M$^3$-VQA基准，包含涉及多个不同实体（来自视觉和文本来源）的多样化多实体问题，要求模型执行顺序和并行的多跳推理，并辅以可追溯的详细证据和精心整理的多模态知识库；评测了16种主流MLLMs在三种设置下（无外部知识、给定金证据、检索增强输入）的性能。
AI.result: 16种主流MLLMs在M$^3$-VQA上表现普遍较差，无外部知识时性能极低；提供精确金证据后性能显著提升；基于推理的智能体检索方法优于启发式检索方法，表明结构化推理对复杂多模态理解至关重要。
AI.conclusion: M$^3$-VQA揭示了当前MLLMs在多实体知识获取和多跳推理方面的显著不足，强调了结构化推理和精确外部知识供给对于推进多模态复杂推理能力的重要性，为后续研究提供了更具挑战性的评估基准。

title: Prefill-Time Intervention for Mitigating Hallucination in Large Vision-Language Models
summary: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) have achieved remarkable progress in visual-textual understanding, yet their reliability is critically undermined by hallucinations, i.e., the generation of factually incorrect or inconsistent responses. While recent studies using steering vectors demonstrated promise in reducing hallucinations, a notable challenge remains: they inadvertently amplify the severity of residual hallucinations. We attribute this to their exclusive focus on the decoding stage, where errors accumulate autoregressively and progressively worsen subsequent hallucinatory outputs. To address this, we propose Prefill-Time Intervention (PTI), a novel steering paradigm that intervenes only once during the prefill stage, enhancing the initial Key-Value (KV) cache before error accumulation occurs. Specifically, PTI is modality-aware, deriving distinct directions for visual and textual representations. This intervention is decoupled to steer keys toward visually-grounded objects and values to filter background noise, correcting hallucination-prone representations at their source. Extensive experiments demonstrate PTI's significant performance in mitigating hallucinations and its generalizability across diverse decoding strategies, LVLMs, and benchmarks. Moreover, PTI is orthogonal to existing decoding-stage methods, enabling plug-and-play integration and further boosting performance. Code is available at: https://github.com/huaiyi66/PTI.

AI.tldr: 本文提出Prefill-Time Intervention (PTI)，一种在预填充阶段对初始KV缓存进行单次、模态感知的干预方法，通过解耦键和值的修正方向来从源头抑制LVLM幻觉，并在多个基准上取得显著效果，且能与现有解码阶段方法正交集成。
AI.motivation: 现有基于steering vector的LVLM幻觉缓解方法仅在解码阶段干预，导致错误在自回归过程中累积并反而放大残差幻觉的严重性。PTI旨在从生成源头——预填充阶段的初始KV缓存——进行干预，在错误累积发生之前矫正易产生幻觉的表示，克服现有方法仅关注解码阶段而忽略误差积累问题的局限。
AI.method: PTI是一种仅在预填充阶段进行一次干预的新范式，通过对初始Key-Value (KV) 缓存施加模态感知的修正来抑制幻觉。具体而言，PTI从视觉和文本表示中分别推导出不同的干预方向，并将干预解耦为两个独立部分：对键（Key）进行引导使其聚焦于视觉上存在（grounded）的对象，对值（Value）进行过滤以降低背景噪声，从而在错误累积的源头矫正易产生幻觉的表示。
AI.result: 大量实验表明，PTI在缓解幻觉方面表现显著，并具有良好的泛化能力，适用于多种解码策略、不同的LVLM架构及多个评测基准。此外，PTI与现有解码阶段方法正交，可作为即插即用模块进一步提升性能。
AI.conclusion: 该研究揭示了仅关注解码阶段干预的固有缺陷，提出了在预填充阶段从源头矫正幻觉表示的有效新范式。PTI的模态感知解耦设计和对KV缓存的双重修正是关键创新，其与现有方法的正交性为幻觉缓解的实际应用提供了灵活且高效的组合方案。

title: FCMBench-Video: Benchmarking Document Video Intelligence
summary: Document understanding is a critical capability in financial credit review, onboarding, and remote verification, where both decision accuracy and evidence traceability matter. Compared with static document images, document videos present a temporally redundant and sequentially unfolding evidence stream, require evidence integration across frames, and preserve acquisition-process cues relevant to authenticity-sensitive and anti-fraud review. We introduce FCMBench-Video, a benchmark for document-video intelligence that evaluates document perception, temporal grounding, and evidence-grounded reasoning under realistic capture conditions. For privacy-compliant yet realistic data at scale, we organize construction as an atomic-acquisition and composition workflow that records reusable single-document clips, applies controlled degradations, and assembles long-form multi-document videos with prescribed temporal spans. FCMBench-Video is built from 495 atomic videos composed into 1,200 long-form videos paired with 11,322 expert-annotated question--answer instances, covering 28 document types over 20s--60s duration tiers and 5,960 Chinese / 5,362 English instances. Evaluations on nine recent Video-MLLMs show that FCMBench-Video provides meaningful separation across systems and capabilities: counting is the most duration-sensitive task, Cross-Document Validation and Evidence-Grounded Selection probe higher-level evidence integration, and Visual Prompt Injection provides a complementary robustness dimension. The overall score distribution is broad and approximately bell-shaped, indicating a benchmark that is neither saturated nor dominated by trivial cases. Together, these results position FCMBench-Video as a reproducible benchmark for tracking Video-MLLM progress on document-video understanding and probing capability boundaries in authenticity-sensitive credit-domain applications.

AI.tldr: 本文提出FCMBench-Video，一个面向文档视频智能理解的多模态基准，涵盖文档感知、时间定位和基于证据的推理三大能力，并基于原子采集与组合工作流在隐私合规条件下构建了大规模、多语种、多时长文档视频数据集，实验表明该基准能有效区分主流Video-MLLM的能力边界。
AI.motivation: 现有文档理解研究多聚焦于静态文档图像，而金融信贷审核等真实性敏感场景中的文档视频具有时间冗余、证据跨帧融合、采集过程蕴含反欺诈线索等独特特性，缺乏一个同时评估文档感知、时间定位和证据级推理能力的标准化视频文档基准。
AI.method: 提出原子采集与组合工作流：先录制可复用的单文档原子视频并施加可控退化，再按指定时间跨度组装成长格式多文档视频。基于495段原子视频构建1,200段长视频，配备11,322个专家标注的问答实例，涵盖28种文档类型、20–60秒时长层级、5,960个中文与5,362个英文实例。
AI.result: 在9个最新Video-MLLM上的评估显示：计数任务对时长最敏感，跨文档验证和基于证据的选择任务探测了更高层次的证据整合能力，视觉提示注入提供了互补的鲁棒性维度；总体分数分布呈近似钟形且范围广泛，表明基准既未饱和也未由简单案例主导。
AI.conclusion: FCMBench-Video作为可复现的文档视频基准，能够有效追踪Video-MLLM在文档视频理解上的进展，并探测模型在信贷领域真实性敏感应用中的能力边界，为后续文档视频智能研究提供了标准化的评估平台。

title: SIEVES: Selective Prediction Generalizes through Visual Evidence Scoring
summary: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) achieve ever-stronger performance on visual-language tasks. Even as traditional visual question answering benchmarks approach saturation, reliable deployment requires satisfying low error tolerances in real-world out-of-distribution (OOD) scenarios. Precisely, selective prediction aims to improve coverage, i.e. the share of inputs the system answers, while adhering to a user-defined risk level. This is typically achieved by assigning a confidence score to each answer and abstaining on those that fall below a certain threshold. To enable reliable generalization, we require reasoner models to produce localized visual evidence while answering, and design a selector that explicitly learns to estimate the quality of the localization provided by the reasoner. We show that SIEVES (Selective Prediction through Visual Evidence Scoring) improves coverage by up to three times on challenging OOD benchmarks (V* Bench, HR-Bench-8k, MME-RealWorld-Lite, VizWiz, and AdVQA), compared to non-grounding baselines. Beyond better generalization to OOD tasks, the design of the SIEVES selector enables transfer to proprietary reasoners without access to their weights or logits, such as o3 and Gemini-3-Pro, providing coverage boosts beyond those attributable to accuracy alone. We highlight that SIEVES generalizes across all five tested OOD datasets and reasoner models (Pixel-Reasoner, o3, and Gemini-3-Pro), without benchmark- or reasoner-specific training or adaptation.

AI.tldr: SIEVES通过显式学习评估推理模型提供的视觉定位质量，实现选择性预测，在多个OOD基准上将覆盖率提升至三倍，且无需针对特定模型或数据集进行适配即可迁移至闭源推理模型。
AI.motivation: 多模态大语言模型在传统VQA基准上趋于饱和，但在现实世界中的分布外场景下难以满足低容错率要求，现有选择性预测方法缺乏对推理模型输出中视觉证据质量的显式评估机制，导致OOD泛化能力不足。
AI.method: SIEVES框架包含两个核心组件：推理模型在回答问题时需生成局部化的视觉证据，选择器则显式学习评估该视觉定位的质量，并据此决定是否对当前输入进行回答或弃权，通过置信度阈值控制风险水平。
AI.result: 在V* Bench、HR-Bench-8k、MME-RealWorld-Lite、VizWiz和AdVQA五个挑战性OOD基准上，SIEVES相比非定位基线将覆盖率提升至三倍；该方法可迁移至o3和Gemini-3-Pro等闭源推理模型，在无需访问模型权重或logits的情况下仍带来超越准确率提升的覆盖增益。
AI.conclusion: SIEVES通过视觉证据评分机制有效提升了多模态模型在OOD场景下的可靠部署能力，其选择器设计具有跨模型、跨数据集的强泛化性，无需针对特定基准或推理模型进行训练或适配即可实现显著性能提升。

title: OmniVTG: A Large-Scale Dataset and Training Paradigm for Open-World Video Temporal Grounding
summary: Video Temporal Grounding (VTG), the task of localizing video segments from text queries, struggles in open-world settings due to limited dataset scale and semantic diversity, causing performance gaps between common and rare concepts. To overcome these limitations, we introduce OmniVTG, a new large-scale dataset for open-world VTG, coupled with a Self-Correction Chain-of-Thought (CoT) training paradigm designed to enhance the grounding capabilities of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). Our OmniVTG is constructed via a novel Semantic Coverage Iterative Expansion pipeline, which first identifies gaps in the vocabulary of existing datasets and collects videos that are highly likely to contain these target concepts. For high-quality annotation, we leverage the insight that modern MLLMs excel at dense captioning more than direct grounding and design a caption-centric data engine to prompt MLLMs to generate dense, timestamped descriptions. Beyond the dataset, we observe that simple supervised finetuning (SFT) is insufficient, as a performance gap between rare and common concepts still persists. We find that MLLMs' video understanding ability significantly surpasses their direct grounding ability. Based on this, we propose a Self-Correction Chain-of-Thought (CoT) training paradigm. We train the MLLM to first predict, then use its understanding capabilities to reflect on and refine its own predictions. This capability is instilled via a three-stage pipeline of SFT, CoT finetuning, and reinforcement learning. Extensive experiments show our approach not only excels at open-world grounding in our OmniVTG dataset but also achieves state-of-the-art zero-shot performance on four existing VTG benchmarks. Code is available at https://github.com/oceanflowlab/OmniVTG.

AI.tldr: 本文提出OmniVTG大规模开放世界视频时序定位数据集，并设计自纠错思维链训练范式，通过语义覆盖迭代扩展管道构建数据集、字幕中心数据引擎生成标注，以及三阶段训练流程，显著提升了多模态大模型在开放世界VTG任务上的性能，在四个现有基准上达到零样本SOTA。
AI.motivation: 现有视频时序定位数据集规模有限、语义多样性不足，导致开放世界场景下常见概念与罕见概念之间存在显著的性能差距；同时，简单的监督微调无法有效弥合这一差距。
AI.method: 本文提出OmniVTG数据集，通过语义覆盖迭代扩展管道识别现有数据集词汇空缺并采集包含目标概念的视频；采用字幕中心数据引擎利用MLLMs强大的密集字幕生成能力来生成带时间戳的描述文本。此外，设计自纠错思维链训练范式（自纠错CoT），包含监督微调、CoT微调和强化学习三阶段，训练MLLM先预测、再基于自身理解能力反思并修正预测结果。
AI.result: 所提方法在OmniVTG数据集上展现出优异的开放世界时序定位能力，并在四个现有VTG基准上取得了零样本SOTA性能。
AI.conclusion: 研究发现MLLMs的视频理解能力显著优于其直接时序定位能力，基于此提出的自纠错CoT范式有效利用了这一差距，为开放世界VTG任务提供了一种新的训练策略，且代码已开源。

title: Instruction-Evidence Contrastive Dual-Stream Decoding for Grounded Vision-Language Reasoning
summary: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) exhibit strong performance in instruction following and open-ended vision-language reasoning, yet they frequently generate fluent outputs that are weakly grounded in visual evidence. Prior works have shown that instruction prompting further worsens this issue by amplifying language priors, especially when the visual signal is uncertain or ambiguous. To address this challenge, we propose a decoding framework that explicitly balances linguistic informativeness and visual faithfulness during generation. Our method, Instruction-Evidence Contrastive Dual-Stream Decoding (IECD2), maintains two parallel probability distributions of tokens at each decoding step: an instruction-driven stream that promotes expressive and informative responses, and an evidence-driven stream that enforces strict grounding in the image. These two streams are adaptively fused using a symmetric KL-based contrast-based gate, which suppresses tokens favored by language priors but unsupported by visual evidence, while preserving them when both distributions agree. We evaluate IECD2 on multiple datasets spanning various generative vision-language reasoning tasks such as captioning and visual question answering, including POPE, MME, VQAv2, AMBER, MS-COCO, and LLaVA-Bench. IECD2 demonstrates consistent improvements in task accuracy and reasoning performance, alongside a substantial reduction in hallucination across all evaluation metrics compared to state-of-the-art decoding approaches.

AI.tldr: 提出指令-证据对比双流解码框架IECD2，通过自适应融合指令驱动与证据驱动两种token概率分布，有效平衡VLMs生成中的语言信息性与视觉忠实性，在多数据集上显著减少幻觉并提升推理性能。
AI.motivation: 视觉语言模型在指令跟随和开放推理中表现优异，但常生成流畅却缺乏视觉依据的输出，且指令提示会放大语言先验偏差，尤其在视觉信号模糊时加剧幻觉问题，现有方法未能有效平衡语言信息性与视觉忠实性之间的冲突。
AI.method: 提出IECD2解码框架，在每一步生成中维护两条并行的token概率分布：指令驱动流促进表达丰富且信息量大的响应，证据驱动流强制与图像内容严格对齐；通过对称KL散度对比门控机制自适应融合两流，抑制被语言先验偏好但缺乏视觉证据支持的token，在两流一致时则保留这些token。
AI.result: 在POPE、MME、VQAv2、AMBER、MS-COCO和LLaVA-Bench等多个视觉推理数据集上进行评估，IECD2在任务准确率和推理性能上取得持续提升，并在所有评估指标上相比现有最先进解码方法大幅降低幻觉率。
AI.conclusion: IECD2通过显式分离并对比指令先验与视觉证据的概率分布，为VLM解码阶段提供了有效校准语言先验偏差的思路，验证了在生成过程中动态平衡语言流畅性与视觉忠实性的可行性和有效性，为减少多模态大模型幻觉提供了新的解码策略。
